devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । अवधार्य विरिञ्चस्य निर्व्यलीकामृतं वचः । प्रहस्य प्रेमगर्भेण तदपाङ्गेन सोऽग्रहीत् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca avadhārya viriñcasya nirvyalīkāmṛtaṃ vacaḥ prahasya premagarbheṇa tadapāṅgena so

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
Having heard Brahtnadeva’s sincere and nectarlike speech, and having laughed (at Brahmā’s naivete to advise astrologically favourable moment to the Lord himself whose form is time, he accepted his (prayer) with a side-glance implying affection.

devanagari: ततः सपत्‍नं मुखतः चरन्तं अकुतोभयम् । जघानोत्पत्य गदया हनौ असुरमक्षजः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ sapat‍naṃ mukhataḥ carantaṃ akutobhayam jaghānotpatya gadayā hanau asuramakṣajaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Then that Ādi-Varāha who was born from Brahmā’s nostrils, jumped at the enemy who was fearlessly moving in front of him, and struck the Asura on his chin by his mace.

devanagari: सा हता तेन गदया विहता भगवत्करात् । विघूर्णित अपतद् रेजे तदद्‍भुतं इवाभवत् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sā hatā tena gadayā vihatā bhagavatkarāt vighūrṇita apatad reje tadad‍bhutaṃ ivābhavat

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: A miracle happened. That (Lord’s) mace struck down by his (Asura’s) mace fell down rolling from the Lord’s hand and the Asura’s valour appeared splendid.

devanagari: स तदा लब्धतीर्थोऽपि न बबाधे निरायुधम् । मानयन् स मृधे धर्मं विष्वक्सेनं प्रकोपयन् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tadā labdhatīrtho mānayan sa mṛdhe dharmaṃ viṣvaksenaṃ prakopayan

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Then although he (the Asura) got an opportunity, he did not strike the Lord who was weaponless. He respected the prescribed code of conduct, in the battle enraging Hari.

devanagari: गदायां अपविद्धायां हाहाकारे विनिर्गते । मानयामास तद् धर्मं सुनाभं चास्मरद्विभुः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: gadāyāṃ apaviddhāyāṃ hāhākāre vinirgate mānayāmāsa tad dharmaṃ sunābhaṃ cāsmaradvibhuḥ
translation: When there was a loud uproar at that snatching away of the mace, the All-pervading Lord appreciated the (Asura’s) righteous conduct, and remembered (mentally commissioned) his Sudarśana-disc (cakra).

devanagari: तं व्यग्रचक्रं दितिपुत्राधमेन । स्वपार्षदमुख्येन विषज्जमानम् । चित्रा वाचोऽतद्विदां खेचराणां । तत्रास्मासन् स्वस्ति तेऽमुं जहीति ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ vyagracakraṃ ditiputrādhamena svapārṣadamukhyena viṣajjamānam citrā vāco tatrāsmāsan svasti te
translation: ‘May you be prosperous’, ‘kill him’. Such were the various shouts (utterances) all around in the sky from the celestial beings who were ignorant of his prowess, when he with his eager Sudarśana was attacked in close quarters by his chief attendant (now born as) the vile son of Diti.

devanagari: स तं निशाम्यात्तरथाङ्गमग्रतो । व्यवस्थितं पद्मपलाशलोचनम् । विलोक्य चामर्ष परिप्लुतेन्द्रियो । रुषा स्वदन्तच्छदमादशच्छ्वसन् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa taṃ niśāmyāttarathāṅgamagrato vyavasthitaṃ padmapalāśalocanam vilokya cāmarṣa pariplutendriyo ruṣā svadantacchadamādaśacchvasan

translation: Observing the lotus-eyed Lord standing before him with the Suḍarśana-ḍisc (ready for discharge) in his hand, he (the Asura) with his senses throbbing with rage, and breathing heavily, bit his lip in rage.

devanagari: करालदंष्ट्रश्चक्षुर्भ्यां सञ्चक्षाणो दहन्निव । अभिप्लुत्य स्वगदया हतोऽसीत्याहनद् हरिम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: karāladaṃṣṭraścakṣurbhyāṃ sañcakṣāṇo dahanniva abhiplutya svagadayā hato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: He (the Asura) of fearful tusks, stared at him with glaring eyes as if to burn him down, and springing upon him, assaulted him with his mace, shouting, ‘You are killed’.

devanagari: पदा सव्येन तां साधो भगवान् यज्ञसूकरः । लीलया मिषतः शत्रोः प्राहरद् वातरंहसम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: padā savyena tāṃ sādho bhagavān yajñasūkaraḥ līlayā miṣataḥ śatroḥ prāharad vātaraṃhasam

translation: Oh pious Vidura! while the enemy was just looking agape, the Lord, as the sacrifice in the Boar form, easily kicked with his left foot the mace which came with the velocity of a stormy wind.

devanagari: आह चायुधमाधत्स्व घटस्व त्वं जिगीषसि । इत्युक्तः स तदा भूयः ताडयन् व्यनदद् भृशम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: āha cāyudhamādhatsva ghaṭasva tvaṃ jigīṣasi ityuktaḥ sa tadā bhūyaḥ tāḍayan vyanadad bhṛśam

translation: And (the Varāha) said, “Take up the weapon. As you wish to conquer, try (again)”. When addressed thus, he (the Asura) struck again, and roared lustily.

devanagari: तां स आपततीं वीक्ष्य भगवान् समवस्थितः । जग्राह लीलया प्राप्तां गरुत्मानिव पन्नगीम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ sa āpatatīṃ vīkṣya bhagavān samavasthitaḥ jagrāha līlayā prāptāṃ garutmāniva pannagīm

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Seeing the mace coming towards him, the Lord stood firm and easily caught hold of the weapon like Garuḍa catching a female serpent.

devanagari: स्वपौरुषे प्रतिहते हतमानो महासुरः । नैच्छद्गदां दीयमानां हरिणा विगतप्रभः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: svapauruṣe pratihate hatamāno mahāsuraḥ naicchadgadāṃ dīyamānāṃ hariṇā vigataprabhaḥ
translation: When his personal valour failed, the great Asura, being humiliated, and splendourless, did not wish to take the mace (though) offered by Hari.

devanagari: जग्राह त्रिशिखं शूलं ज्वलज्ज्वलनलोलुपम् । यज्ञाय धृतरूपाय विप्रायाभिचरन् यथा ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: jagrāha triśikhaṃ śūlaṃ jvalajjvalanalolupam yajñāya dhṛtarūpāya viprāyābhicaran yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: He took up a trident spear, eager to envelop (eat up) everything like a flaming fire, and aimed it at the chest of Viṣṇu who had assumed the form of sacrifice like the use of black magic against a pious Brāhmaṇa.

devanagari: तदोजसा दैत्यमहाभटार्पितं । चकासदन्तःख उदीर्णदीधिति । चक्रेण चिच्छेद निशातनेमिना । हरिर्यथा तार्क्ष्यपतत्रमुज्झितम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tadojasā daityamahābhaṭārpitaṃ cakāsadantaḥkha udīrṇadīdhiti cakreṇa ciccheda niśātaneminā hariryathā tārkṣyapatatramujjhitam

[nominative single from √ ujjh class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ ujjh class 6 verb]

translation: Just as Indra cut down the feather thrown out by Garuḍa[1], he (Varāha), with his disc of sharp edge Cut asunder the trident, forcibly hurled by the great Daitya warrior—the trident of extreme refulgence which shone through the sky (as it darted towards Hari).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
The comparison is between the shining Triśūla darting through the sky and the refulgent feather of Garuḍa falling through the sky illumining it on its way down. This refers to the incident when Garuḍa was carrying away Amṛta (nectar), Indra came in the way and hurled his Vajra (thunderbolt) at him. To respect the bones of the sage Dadhīca of which Vajra was made, Garuḍa dropped a feather and told Indra that he was not affected by the impact of Vajra but he dropped the feather out of respect for sage Dadhīca—Mahābhārata I-33.18-23.

devanagari: वृक्णे स्वशूले बहुधारिणा हरेः । प्रत्येत्य विस्तीर्णमुरो विभूतिमत् । प्रवृद्धरोषः स कठोरमुष्टिना । नदन् प्रहृत्यान्तरधीयतासुरः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛkṇe svaśūle bahudhāriṇā hareḥ pratyetya vistīrṇamuro vibhūtimat pravṛddharoṣaḥ sa kaṭhoramuṣṭinā nadan prahṛtyāntaradhīyatāsuraḥ

translation: When his trident was shattered to pieces by Hari’s disc (Sudarśana-cakra), the Asura got extremely enraged. Shouting lustily he confronted Hari and dealt a punch with his hard fist on his broad magnificent[2] chest and disappeared.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
vibhūtimat—the abode of Lakṣmī (Bhāgavata Candrikā)

devanagari: तेनेत्थमाहतः क्षत्तः भगवान् आदिसूकरः । नाकम्पत मनाक्क्वापि स्रजा हत इव द्विपः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tenetthamāhataḥ kṣattaḥ bhagavān ādisūkaraḥ nākampata manākkvāpi srajā hata iva dvipaḥ

translation: Oh Vidura, Lord Adi-Varāha who was thus struck, did not move even slightly like an elephant struck by a garland.

devanagari: अथोरुधासृजन् मायां योगमायेश्वरे हरौ । यां विलोक्य प्रजास्त्रस्ता मेनिरेऽस्योपसंयमम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: athorudhāsṛjan māyāṃ yogamāyeśvare harau yāṃ vilokya prajāstrastā menire

translation: Then he created and used different forms of his black magic against Hari, the Lord of Yoga Māyā. Seeing it (Asura’s māyā) all creatures got panicky and thought that the end of the world (pralaya) was imminent.

devanagari: प्रववुर्वायवश्चण्डाः तमः पांसवमैरयन् । दिग्भ्यो निपेतुर्ग्रावाणः क्षेपणैः प्रहिता इव ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: pravavurvāyavaścaṇḍāḥ tamaḥ pāṃsavamairayan digbhyo nipeturgrāvāṇaḥ kṣepaṇaiḥ prahitā iva
translation: Terrible stormy winds began to blow and spread darkness of dust. Volleys of stones as if discharged from slings, fell from all quarters.

devanagari: द्यौर्नष्टभगणाभ्रौघैः सविद्युत् स्तनयित्‍नुभिः । वर्षद्भिः पूयकेशासृग् विण्मूत्रास्थीनि चासकृत् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: dyaurnaṣṭabhagaṇābhraughaiḥ savidyut stanayit‍nubhiḥ varṣadbhiḥ pūyakeśāsṛg viṇmūtrāsthīni cāsakṛt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 4 verb]

translation: The sky, being covered with clouds accompanied by lightning-flashes and thundering and pouring frequently pus, hair, blood, excretion, urine and bones, seemed devoid of luminaries.

devanagari: गिरयः प्रत्यदृश्यन्त नानायुधमुचोऽनघ । दिग्वाससो यातुधान्यः शूलिन्यो मुक्तमूर्धजाः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: girayaḥ pratyadṛśyanta nānāyudhamuco digvāsaso yātudhānyaḥ śūlinyo muktamūrdhajāḥ
translation: Oh sinless Vidura, the mountains appeared to shower various weapons[3] and naked female Rākṣasas with their hair let loose, appeared with spears.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
VC. takes this as qualifying Rākṣasa women—‘Naked Rākṣasa women discharging various kinds of weapon’.

devanagari: बहुभिर्यक्षरक्षोभिः पत्त्यश्व रथकुञ्जरैः । आततायिभिरुत्सृष्टा हिंस्रा वाचोऽतिवैशसाः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: bahubhiryakṣarakṣobhiḥ pattyaśva rathakuñjaraiḥ ātatāyibhirutsṛṣṭā hiṃsrā vāco
translation: Very harsh and murderous cries (such as ‘cut down, break down, etc.) were shouted out by a host of blood-thirsty Yakṣas, Rākṣasas, foot-soldiers, (riders on) horses, chariots and elephants.

devanagari: प्रादुष्कृतानां मायानां आसुरीणां विनाशयत् । सुदर्शनास्त्रं भगवान् प्रायुङ्क्त दयितं त्रिपात् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: prāduṣkṛtānāṃ māyānāṃ āsurīṇāṃ vināśayat sudarśanāstraṃ bhagavān prāyuṅkta dayitaṃ tripāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: Lord (Varāha) of three feet[4] discharged his favourite missile Sudarśana destroying the Asura type of black magic manifested (there).
purport:
Footnote [4]:
This is the incarnation of Yajña-Varāha or Boar which was sacrifice incarnate. The three savanas are regarded as the three feet of Yajña. Hence this attribute is applied to Viṣṇu as Varāha.—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Subodhinī
Padaratnāvalī states: ‘Amṛta, Kṣema and Abhaya’ are the three feet of Lord. In Puruṣasūkta Puruṣa is tripād.

devanagari: तदा दितेः समभवत् सहसा हृदि वेपथुः । स्मरन्त्या भर्तुः आदेशं स्तनात् च असृक् प्रसुस्रुवे ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tadā diteḥ samabhavat sahasā hṛdi vepathuḥ smarantyā bhartuḥ ādeśaṃ stanāt ca asṛk prasusruve

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [ablative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Simultaneously there was a sudden trembling in the heart of Diti who remembered her husband’s words and blood oozed out of her breast.

devanagari: विनष्टासु स्वमायासु भूयश्चाव्रज्य केशवम् । रुषोपगूहमानोऽमुं ददृशेऽवस्थितं बहिः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: vinaṣṭāsu svamāyāsu bhūyaścāvrajya keśavam ruṣopagūhamāno
translation: When his magical forces were totally destroyed, he again approached Keśava (Viṣṇu), and tried angrily to crush him in the clasp of his arms, but found him outside his clasp.

devanagari: तं मुष्टिभिर्विनिघ्नन्तं वज्रसारैः अधोक्षजः । करेण कर्णमूलेऽहन् यथा त्वाष्ट्रं मरुत्पतिः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ muṣṭibhirvinighnantaṃ vajrasāraiḥ adhokṣajaḥ kareṇa karṇamūle

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: While he was dealing blows with his adamantlike (hard) fists to Viṣṇu, he hit the Asura at the root of his ear by his hand (i.e. foreleg) as Indra, Lord of Maruts, did to Vṛtra.

devanagari: स आहतो विश्वजिता ह्यवज्ञया । परिभ्रमद्गात्र उदस्तलोचनः । विशीर्णबाह्वङ्घ्रिशिरोरुहोऽपतद् । यथा नगेन्द्रो लुलितो नभस्वता ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa āhato viśvajitā hyavajñayā paribhramadgātra udastalocanaḥ viśīrṇabāhvaṅghriśiroruho yathā nagendro lulito nabhasvatā
translation: By the casual blow of the conqueror of the universe (Viṣṇu), the Asura fell like a giant tree unrooted by a stormy wind. His body was rolling about. His eye-balls fell out. His arms, feet and hair lay shattered.

devanagari: क्षितौ शयानं तमकुण्ठवर्चसं । करालदंष्ट्रं परिदष्टदच्छदम् । अजादयो वीक्ष्य शशंसुरागता । अहो इमं को नु लभेत संस्थितिम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣitau śayānaṃ tamakuṇṭhavarcasaṃ karāladaṃṣṭraṃ paridaṣṭadacchadam ajādayo vīkṣya śaśaṃsurāgatā aho imaṃ ko nu labheta saṃsthitim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ kṣī class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṣī class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṣī class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṣī class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṣī class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṣī class 5 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṣī class 9 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṣī class 9 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṣī class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ śī class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ śī class 2 verb]
[optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: Brahmā and others (sages) who came there saw the Asura of terrific tusks and lips bitten, lying on the ground, but with undiminished lustre. They exclaimed in praise, ‘Oh who could attain to such (type of) death!’

devanagari: यं योगिनो योगसमाधिना रहो । ध्यायन्ति लिङ्गादसतो मुमुक्षया । तस्यैष दैत्यऋषभः पदाहतो । मुखं प्रपश्यन् तनुमुत्ससर्ज ह ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: yaṃ yogino yogasamādhinā raho dhyāyanti liṅgādasato mumukṣayā tasyaiṣa daityaṛṣabhaḥ padāhato mukhaṃ prapaśyan tanumutsasarja ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single], [imperative passive first single]

translation: This wicked Daitya kicked by Viṣṇu’s fore-leg, gave up his body while looking at the face of Viṣṇu on whom the yogins meditate by samādhi-yoga in solitude with a desire to get liberation from this Liṅga-śarīra,[5] enveloping the Soul.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
In Vedānta philosophy this subtle body is regarded as the indestructible original of tḥe gross or visible body. ASD. 816.

devanagari: एतौ तौ पार्षदावस्य शापाद् यातौ असद्गतिम् । पुनः कतिपयैः स्थानं प्रपत्स्येते ह जन्मभिः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: etau tau pārṣadāvasya śāpād yātau asadgatim punaḥ katipayaiḥ sthānaṃ prapatsyete ha janmabhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ yā class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ yā class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ yā class 2 verb]

translation: These two Viṣṇu’s attendants who have come down to evil births due to a curse, will again be reinstated to their (former) status, after some births in this world.

devanagari: देवा ऊचुः । नमो नमस्तेऽखिलयज्ञतन्तवे । स्थितौ गृहीतामलसत्त्वमूर्तये । दिष्ट्या हतोऽयं जगतामरुन्तुदः । त्वत् पादभक्त्या वयमीश निर्वृताः ...
english_devnagari: devā ūcuḥ namo namaste sthitau gṛhītāmalasattvamūrtaye diṣṭyā hato tvat pādabhaktyā vayamīśa nirvṛtāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation:
Gods said:
“Oh Lord, we bow to you again and again. You who are the cause of extension of Yajña[6] assumed the form of pure Sattva attribute for the maintenance (and protection) of the world. It is a matter of joy that this Daitya who was a scourge (lit. afflictor) of the world is killed. We are quite happy in the devotion of your feet.”
purport:
Footnote [6]:
yajña-tantave: (i) the source or promoter of yajña (sacrifice)—Bhāgavata Candrikā, Sārārthadarśinī,
(ii) who are yourself the institution of yajña (incarnate)—Subodhinī

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । एवं हिरण्याक्षमसह्यविक्रमं । स सादयित्वा हरिरादिसूकरः । जगाम लोकं स्वमखण्डितोत्सवं । समीडितः पुष्करविष्टरादिभिः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca evaṃ hiraṇyākṣamasahyavikramaṃ sa sādayitvā harirādisūkaraḥ jagāma lokaṃ svamakhaṇḍitotsavaṃ samīḍitaḥ puṣkaraviṣṭarādibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
In this way, having killed Hiraṇyākṣa of irresistible valour, Hari, the Primitive Boar, being praised by Brahmā and other gods, retired to his region (Vaikuṇṭha) of uninterrupted bliss.

devanagari: मया यथानूक्तमवादि ते हरेः । कृतावतारस्य सुमित्र चेष्टितम् । यथा हिरण्याक्ष उदारविक्रमो । महामृधे क्रीडनवन्निराकृतः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: mayā yathānūktamavādi te hareḥ kṛtāvatārasya sumitra ceṣṭitam yathā hiraṇyākṣa udāravikramo mahāmṛdhe krīḍanavannirākṛtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ ceṣṭ ], [accusative single from √ ceṣṭ ]

translation: Oh good friend, Hiraṇyākṣa of great valour was disposed of (killed) like a toy in a big battle. This exploit of Hari who assumed the boar incarnation has been narrated to yuo by me as described to me by my teacher.

devanagari: सूत उवाच । इति कौषारवाख्यातां आश्रुत्य भगवत्कथाम् । क्षत्तानन्दं परं लेभे महाभागवतो द्विज ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvāca iti kauṣāravākhyātāṃ āśrutya bhagavatkathām kṣattānandaṃ paraṃ lebhe mahābhāgavato dvija

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation:
Sūta said:
Oh Brāhmaṇa (Śaunaka), on hearing this episode of the Lord from Maitreya, Vidura, the great devotee of the Lord was highly delighted.

devanagari: अन्येषां पुण्यश्लोकानां उद्दामयशसां सताम् । उपश्रुत्य भवेन्मोदः श्रीवत्साङ्कस्य किं पुनः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: anyeṣāṃ puṇyaślokānāṃ uddāmayaśasāṃ satām upaśrutya bhavenmodaḥ śrīvatsāṅkasya kiṃ punaḥ
translation: How much more shall we be delighted to hear the deeds of Lord Viṣṇu when we feel such a joy at hearing the deeds of the pious persons of sanctifying reputation and highly glorious fame?

devanagari: यो गजेन्द्रं झषग्रस्तं ध्यायन्तं चरणाम्बुजम् । क्रोशन्तीनां करेणूनां कृच्छ्रतोऽमोचयद्द्रुतम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: yo gajendraṃ jhaṣagrastaṃ dhyāyantaṃ caraṇāmbujam krośantīnāṃ kareṇūnāṃ kṛcchrato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ gras class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ gras class 1 verb]

translation: He (Viṣṇu) instantaneously rescued from danger the big elephant which being caught by a crocodile, meditated of his lotuslike feet while the she-elephants(his companions) were trumpeting.

devanagari: तं सुखाराध्यमृजुभिः अनन्यशरणैर्नृभिः । कृतज्ञः को न सेवेत दुराराध्यं असाधुभिः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ sukhārādhyamṛjubhiḥ ananyaśaraṇairnṛbhiḥ kṛtajñaḥ ko na seveta durārādhyaṃ asādhubhiḥ
translation: What grateful person will not serve him who is easily propitiated by straightforward persons completely depending on him but difficult for propitiation to the wicked.

devanagari: यो वै हिरण्याक्षवधं महाद्‍भुतं । विक्रीडितं कारणसूकरात्मनः । श्रृणोति गायत्यनुमोदतेऽञ्जसा । विमुच्यते ब्रह्मवधादपि द्विजाः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: yo vai hiraṇyākṣavadhaṃ mahād‍bhutaṃ vikrīḍitaṃ kāraṇasūkarātmanaḥ śrṛṇoti gāyatyanumodate vimucyate brahmavadhādapi dvijāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Oh Brāhmaṇas, a person becomes free from the sin of killing a Brāhmaṇa, if he (the sinner) hears, sings or takes delight in the episode of his miraculous action of killing Hiraṇyākṣa—a sport of Hari who assumed the boar form for the purpose (of lifting up the earth).

devanagari: एतन् महापुण्यमलं पवित्रं । धन्यं यशस्यं पदमायुराशिषाम् । प्राणेन्द्रियाणां युधि शौर्यवर्धनं । नारायणोऽन्ते गतिरङ्ग श्रृण्वताम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: etan mahāpuṇyamalaṃ pavitraṃ dhanyaṃ yaśasyaṃ padamāyurāśiṣām prāṇendriyāṇāṃ yudhi śauryavardhanaṃ nārāyaṇo
translation:
This (episode of Hari) is highly meritorious, extremely sanctifying, conferring wealth, fame, longevity, blessings; in battles it protects life and organs of senses and inspires heroic spirit. The listeners of this ultimately attain to Nārāyaṇa as the final resort.
